description: All foundation work — excavation through slab cure and anchor bolt verification — is complete, inspected, and approved before framing begins.

categories.name: Pre-Pour Verification
items: Building permit posted on-site and foundation inspection scheduled with Baldwin County Building Department
Foundation layout staked and verified against approved engineering drawings; setbacks confirmed with survey pins
Excavation depth matches engineered footing schedule; verified by superintendent with tape measure on all corners and load-bearing points
Soil bearing capacity confirmed by geo-tech or visual inspection per soils report on file; no soft spots, organic material, or standing water in bearing zones
Vapor barrier (minimum 6-mil poly, 10-mil preferred for $1M+ builds) installed, lapped minimum 12 inches at seams, and sealed at penetrations
All underground plumbing sleeves and conduit stubs set per MEP plan; locations confirmed with plumber before pour
Termite pre-treatment (soil treatment) completed by licensed pest control company; certificate issued and on file per Alabama Code

categories.name: Formwork & Reinforcement
items: Form dimensions match approved foundation plan; corners square to within 1/8 inch per 10 feet diagonal check
Rebar size, spacing, and lap lengths match structural engineering stamped drawings (e.g., #5 bars at 16" O.C. each way where specified)
Rebar clearance from earth maintained at minimum 3 inches on all sides; plastic chairs or equivalent used throughout — no wire-tied dirt contact
Monolithic thickened edges formed to engineered depth (typically 12–18 inches for Baldwin County coastal zone loads)
Post-tension cables or additional reinforcement installed per engineer's layout if post-tension slab specified; stressing pockets formed at correct locations
All penetrations (plumbing, electrical, HVAC) sleeved and blocked out per plan; no field-cut rebar around penetrations without engineer sign-off
Baldwin County foundation/footing inspection completed and inspection card signed before concrete is ordered

categories.name: Concrete Pour & Placement
items: Concrete mix design matches engineer's specification (minimum 3,000 PSI at 28 days; 4,000 PSI for coastal/high-humidity exposure preferred on $1M+ builds)
Concrete delivery tickets reviewed on arrival; mix, slump (max 4 inches without admixture approval), and load time verified
Concrete placed and consolidated without cold joints; vibrated at maximum 18-inch intervals around rebar and form edges
Slab finished to specified flatness tolerance: FF 25 / FL 20 minimum (F-number spec or straightedge max 3/16-inch gap under 10-foot straightedge)
Anchor bolt schedule installed per structural drawings — size, embedment depth, projection height, and spacing verified against framing bolt pattern before concrete sets
Slab elevation checked at minimum four corners and center with builder's level; maximum deviation from design elevation ±3/8 inch
Concrete test cylinders taken per ACI 301 (minimum one set of 3 cylinders per 50 CY or per day's pour); ticket numbers logged in project file

categories.name: Cure & Protection
items: Curing compound applied immediately after final finish, or wet-cure method (burlap + poly) maintained for minimum 7 days per ACI 308
Slab protected from rain damage, UV, and foot traffic for minimum 24 hours; form removal not before 24 hours or per engineer's direction
28-day break strength confirmed from cylinder tests at ≥ specified PSI; test results on file before framing loads are applied
Post-tension cables stressed (if applicable) at 75% design strength per engineer's schedule; stressing log signed by contractor and on file
Slab inspected for cracks after cure: hairline shrinkage cracks noted and photographed; any crack wider than 1/16 inch or with vertical offset escalated to structural engineer before framing

categories.name: Anchor Bolts & Sill Plate Prep
items: All anchor bolt locations verified against approved framing plan; bolts plumb (within 1/8 inch), correct projection above slab for specified sill plate thickness plus nut and washer
Anchor bolt thread condition inspected; no damaged threads, cement fouling, or misalignment that would prevent full nut engagement
Sill plate pressure-treated species and grade confirmed per plans (minimum PT 0.40 ACQ for ground contact/slab interface in Baldwin County coastal environment)
Termite shield (metal or approved barrier) specified on drawings installed under sill plate where required by plan or code
Slab surface at sill plate bearing zone ground or shimmed flat to within 1/8 inch over any 3-foot run; no rocking or gap that would cause point loading on framing

categories.name: Inspections & Sign-Off
items: Baldwin County final foundation inspection passed; inspection card signed and photographed
Superintendent final walk of slab: all penetration sleeves capped, no debris embedded in surface, no rebar tips exposed
Slab elevation survey completed and elevations recorded in project file; superintendent and PM have both reviewed and approved
Foundation phase photos uploaded to project Drive folder (pre-pour reinforcement, anchor bolts, finish surface, inspection card)
Any field deviations from engineered drawings documented, RFI issued, and engineer written response on file before framing begins
Foundation phase marked complete in project schedule; framing crew start date confirmed with foreman
